description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated human figure, possibly of ancient Egyptian origin, carved from a reddish-brown stone. The figure is shown with arms resting on a rectangular tablet or stela, which features a smaller, detailed relief of another seated figure within a recessed niche. The back of the sculpture displays vertical lines of inscribed text, likely hieroglyphics, running down the stela. The figure sits on a roughly hewn base that transitions into a smooth, rectangular plinth. The facial features are stylized, with a serene expression and a headdress or hair cap. The overall surface is matte with visible chisel marks and weathering, suggesting age and historical significance.
description: A reddish-brown stone sculpture of a seated human figure holding a tablet with a smaller relief figure and inscribed text on the back. The piece features stylized facial details and a weathered, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the seated figure holding a tablet with a smaller relief figure in a niche and clear facial features.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the figure's arm resting on the tablet and the depth of the niche, with the base and plinth visible.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals vertical lines of inscribed text on the back of the tablet and the rounded back of the figure.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the arm's position and the thickness of the tablet, with the base and plinth in profile.
attribute_subject_matter: seated human figure with inscribed tablet and relief
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
